intent: Pin the trust-critical auth/tenancy contract with executable, red-able held-out tests before any code exists.
key_changes_made: test_get_or_404_cross_company_is_404_not_403_not_redirect: a cross-company object id via get_or_404 raises HTTP 404, identical to a nonexistent id — never 403, never a redirect
test_get_or_404_returns_own_company_object: get_or_404 returns the caller's own-company object (positive control so the 404s are not unconditional)
test_get_or_404_ignores_a_company_id_kwarg_if_accepted: a caller-supplied company_id can never unlock another tenant's object; scoping uses only user.company_id
test_company_query_only_returns_own_company_rows: company_query yields only rows where company_id == user.company_id (no cross-tenant leakage)
test_session_payload_carries_no_role_or_company: the session identifies the user by user_id alone — no role/company/email smuggled into the payload
test_logout_without_csrf_is_400_and_session_survives: POST /logout with no csrf_token field -> 400 and the session is NOT cleared
test_logout_with_wrong_csrf_is_400_and_session_survives: POST /logout with an incorrect token -> 400, session survives
test_logout_with_another_sessions_csrf_is_400: a csrf token belonging to a different session is rejected (400); tokens are per-session
test_logout_with_valid_csrf_succeeds: positive control — a valid token yields 303 to /login and clears the session
test_settings_post_without_csrf_is_400_and_no_mutation: mutating POST /settings without csrf -> 400 and the company row is unchanged
test_settings_post_with_wrong_csrf_is_400_and_no_mutation: mutating POST /settings with a wrong token -> 400, company unchanged

test_signup_creates_exactly_one_company_and_one_owner: signup creates exactly one Company and one owner User, stores email lowercase, and hashes the password with bcrypt ($2, verify round-trips, no plaintext in the hash)
test_signup_password_never_appears_plaintext_in_any_column: the raw password appears verbatim in no User column
test_short_password_rejected_and_nothing_created: a <8-char password re-renders the form at 200 and creates nothing
test_password_length_boundary_eight_is_accepted: an exactly-8-char password is accepted (pins the min-length boundary / off-by-one)
test_invalid_timezone_rejected_and_nothing_created: a non-IANA timezone re-renders at 200 and creates nothing

test_duplicate_email_rejected_globally_nothing_created: a second signup with an existing email (a new would-be company) is rejected at 200 and creates no second User or Company (global uniqueness across companies)
test_duplicate_email_detection_is_case_insensitive: duplicate-email detection is case-insensitive
test_full_cycle_signup_logout_login: signup -> logout -> login round-trips through TestClient with the specified 303 redirects, and home displays the company name
test_login_email_is_case_insensitive: login matches the stored (lowercased) email case-insensitively
test_wrong_password_and_unknown_email_are_indistinguishable: wrong-password and unknown-email logins return the SAME generic visible error at 200 (no user enumeration)
test_wrong_password_does_not_establish_a_session: a failed login establishes no session
test_settings_forbidden_for_crew: an authenticated crew user gets 403 from GET /settings
test_settings_allowed_for_owner: positive control — an owner gets 200 from GET /settings (so the crew 403 is role-based, not blanket)
test_unauthenticated_home_redirects_303_to_login: unauthenticated GET / -> 303 to /login
test_unauthenticated_settings_redirects_303_to_login_not_403: unauthenticated GET /settings -> 303 to /login (redirect, not 403 — distinguishing missing-auth from wrong-role)
key_learnings: No route in THIS task accepts an object id (customer/schedule models come later), so the CRITICAL cross-tenant-404 invariant has no HTTP surface here; it is pinned directly on the get_or_404/company_query helpers that every later route depends on.

The spec's 'Session payload: {user_id} ONLY' coexists with 'generate csrf_token into the session', so a live session legitimately holds BOTH user_id and csrf_token; the enforceable invariant is 'no role/company smuggled in', not 'exactly one key'.

Environment gotchas an implementer will hit in this same runtime: passlib 1.7.4 is broken against the installed bcrypt 5.0 (bcrypt has no __about__), and Starlette's Jinja2 TemplateResponse now requires the (request, name, context) signature — both surfaced while building the verification sketch.

spec_ambiguities.text: What are the exact HTML form field <input name=...> POST parameter names for signup/login/settings (e.g. is the company field 'company_name' or 'company', and the person field 'name' or 'owner_name')?
Do POST /signup and POST /login enforce CSRF (400 on missing/invalid token), or is CSRF enforced only on authenticated mutating POSTs (logout/settings)?
Is global email uniqueness enforced by a DB UNIQUE constraint on User.email, or only by an application-level duplicate check in POST /signup (or both)?
Is it acceptable for the gl_session cookie to carry csrf_token alongside user_id, given the spec says the payload is 'user_id ONLY' yet also stores the CSRF token in the session?

summary: Authored 26 held-out adversarial tests across 4 files (tenancy, csrf, signup, login/session) for GL2-auth-tenancy from the spec alone, with no implementation present. The CRITICAL cross-tenant invariant (acceptance #3) is pinned directly on the get_or_404/company_query helpers because this task ships no route that takes an object id. All signup/login/settings interactions are driven naming-agnostically (form fields discovered from the rendered HTML, email/password/timezone detected by input type, every text field filled with a valid value), so the corpus passes under any reasonable choice of form field names. I built a faithful throwaway sketch (deleted) to confirm every test goes GREEN against a correct impl (25 pass, 1 correct skip) and RED against 19 targeted single-behavior mutations covering all six acceptance criteria and every adversarial-mandate bullet — no tautologies, no false-reds. All four spec ambiguities I encountered were written to pass under BOTH readings, so none park this task.
